summary: Surah An-Naml recounts the stories of Musa, Sulaiman, and other prophets, highlighting their miracles and wisdom. It describes the Queen of Sheba’s encounter with Sulaiman and her acceptance of faith. The surah emphasizes Allah’s signs in creation, the folly of idolatry, and the certainty of resurrection and accountability. It warns against arrogance and denial, and urges gratitude, faith, and reliance on Allah. The surah assures believers of reward and safety, and warns disbelievers of the consequences of their actions.
